name: Business Website
professionalExpectations: Clear statement of what the business does, for whom, and where, visible without scrolling.
A visible primary contact path on every page: phone, form, or both.
Real service descriptions rather than filler copy.
Proof elements: past work, testimonials, certifications, or service-area credibility.
Fast load on mobile connections and correct rendering on phone-width screens.
Working links, working forms, no placeholder text in published output.
Consistent branding: one logo treatment, one palette, one type scale.
minimumQualityBar: Every page passes mobile and desktop layout review.
Primary call to action is reachable within one click from any page.
No lorem ipsum, broken images, or empty sections at publish time.
Forms validate input and clearly confirm submission.
Page titles and descriptions are unique and descriptive.
Local validation passes before any PR merges.
commonModules: Public information pages for offer, proof, about, and contact paths.
Content sections for services, process, testimonials or proof, frequently asked questions, and calls to action.
SEO metadata, sitemap planning, structured headings, and conversion path review.
Contact surface prepared as a non-live shell until owner-approved routing, storage, or messaging exists.
commonPages: Service area or locations when the business is local
uxExpectations: Navigation stays under seven top-level items.
One primary call to action per page, visually dominant.
Readable type contrast and sizes on mobile without zooming.
Content ordered by visitor priority: what, proof, how to contact.
No autoplaying audio, intrusive popups, or scroll hijacking.
seoBasics: Unique title and meta description per page.
One H1 per page matching search intent.
Descriptive URLs and alt text on meaningful images.
Local business schema markup when the business serves a local area.
Sitemap and robots configuration generated at build time.
conversionBasics: Primary conversion action defined before design starts.
Contact options match how the audience actually reaches out.
Proof placed adjacent to calls to action.
Forms ask only for fields the business will actually use.
Thank-you or confirmation state defined for every conversion path.

components: Static or lightweight framework site kept in a GitHub repository.
GitHub as source of truth with CI validation before merge.
Netlify as the future hosting target, deploys only after owner approval.
Existing domain or subdomain, changed only with owner approval.
No database unless the site genuinely needs dynamic data.
Base44 optional for visual prototyping only, never source of truth.
recommendedStack.notes: Prefer the existing stack first. A static site meets the quality bar for most business websites at near-zero cost.

phases.name: Scope and information architecture
goals: Define audience, offer, service area, and the primary conversion action.
Define the page list and content outline from owner-provided requirements.
Record assumptions and open questions, maximum three critical unknowns.

phases.name: Build and content
goals: Implement pages against the information architecture in a GitHub repository.
Write real copy grounded in owner-provided business facts.
Pass local validation and CI on every PR.

phases.name: Quality pass
goals: Review against this archetype's quality checklist.
Fix mobile, performance, SEO, and conversion gaps.
Record a quality score and lesson candidates.

phases.name: Approval-gated launch
goals: Prepare deployment plan and rollback notes.
Stop for owner approval before any hosting, deploy, or domain action.

knownPitfalls: Building pages before the conversion goal is defined.
Filler copy that describes no real service or area.
Desktop-only design reviewed on a wide monitor only.
Adding a CMS or database a brochure site does not need.
Treating deployment as part of the build instead of an owner-gated step.
Skipping the proof section because content is not ready.
approvalGates: Repository creation is a live connector action and requires owner approval.
Any Netlify staging or production deploy requires owner approval.
Any domain or DNS change requires owner approval.
Any paid tool, stock asset purchase, or paid API requires owner approval.
qualityChecklist: Value proposition is clear within five seconds on the home page.
All archetype common pages exist or were consciously excluded in the plan.
Primary call to action works and is one click away everywhere.
Mobile layout reviewed at phone width.
SEO basics applied: titles, descriptions, H1s, alt text, sitemap.
Forms validated and confirmation states present.
No placeholder content in the final output.
Cost stayed within Cost OS limits.
whatToBuildFirst: Static first-pass pages with owner-approved business facts and clean responsive layout.
Reusable sections for services, proof, process, and contact intent.
Local validation and build checks before hosting or domain work.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Custom CMS or database before the owner confirms editing needs.
User accounts, dashboards, or portals unless the website is explicitly part of an app.
Advanced animation, personalization, analytics, or marketing automation before the core offer is clear.
Payment, booking, messaging, or form delivery integrations before approval gates are complete.
stopConditions: Stop before creating or modifying a live deployment, domain, DNS record, paid service, credential, or production data path.
Stop before connecting analytics, forms, payments, booking, email, SMS, or CRM integrations without owner approval.
Stop if the requested work would touch Lead Gen production or the AI Receptionist repo outside an approved project scope.
notes: Grounded in the construction website dry runs of 2026-07-03. This record is knowledge only; it authorizes nothing.
